*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: cache.go
+package main
+
+import (
+	"sync"
+	"time"
+)
+
+// Cache implements a simple in‑memory key/value store with optional TTL
+// (time‑to‑live) and a maximum capacity. When the capacity is reached the
+// oldest entry (based on expiry time) is evicted. Expired entries are also
+// removed lazily on Set/Get and proactively via evictExpired().
+type Cache struct {
+	mu       sync.RWMutex
+	items    map[string]cacheItem
+	capacity int
+	ttl      time.Duration
+}
+
+type cacheItem struct {
+	value  interface{}
+	expiry time.Time // zero value means no expiration
+}
+
+// New creates a new Cache with the given capacity and TTL.
+// A ttl of zero means entries never expire.
+func New(capacity int, ttl time.Duration) *Cache {
+	if capacity < 0 {
+		capacity = 0
+	}
+	return &Cache{
+		items:    make(map[string]cacheItem),
+		capacity: capacity,
+		ttl:      ttl,
+	}
+}
+
+// Set stores a value under the given key. It overwrites any existing entry.
+// Proper locking is used: a write lock for the mutation, and TTL is calculated
+// using time.Now().Add (not Sub). If the cache exceeds its capacity, the oldest
+// entry is evicted.
+func (c *Cache) Set(key string, value interface{}) {
+	c.mu.Lock()
+	defer c.mu.Unlock()
+
+	// Evict expired items first to possibly make room.
+	c.evictExpiredLocked()
+
+	// If we are at capacity (and capacity > 0), evict the oldest entry.
+	if c.capacity > 0 && len(c.items) >= c.capacity {
+		c.evictOldestLocked()
+	}
+
+	exp := time.Time{}
+	if c.ttl > 0 {
+		exp = time.Now().Add(c.ttl)
+	}
+	c.items[key] = cacheItem{value: value, expiry: exp}
+}
+
+// Get retrieves a value for the given key. It returns the value and a bool
+// indicating whether the key was present and not expired.
+func (c *Cache) Get(key string) (interface{}, bool) {
+	c.mu.RLock()
+	item, ok := c.items[key]
+	c.mu.RUnlock()
+	if !ok {
+		return nil, false
+	}
+	// Check expiration without holding the lock for long.
+	if !item.expiry.IsZero() && time.Now().After(item.expiry) {
+		// Lazy removal of expired entry.
+		c.mu.Lock()
+		// Re‑check existence under write lock to avoid race.
+		if cur, present := c.items[key]; present && cur.expiry == item.expiry {
+			delete(c.items, key)
+		}
+		c.mu.Unlock()
+		return nil, false
+	}
+	return item.value, true
+}
+
+// evictExpired removes all entries whose TTL has passed.
+// It assumes the caller holds the appropriate lock; a public wrapper is provided
+// for convenience that acquires a write lock.
+func (c *Cache) evictExpired() {
+	c.mu.Lock()
+	defer c.mu.Unlock()
+	c.evictExpiredLocked()
+}
+
+// evictExpiredLocked is the internal implementation that expects the write lock
+// to already be held.
+func (c *Cache) evictExpiredLocked() {
+	now := time.Now()
+	for k, it := range c.items {
+		if !it.expiry.IsZero() && now.After(it.expiry) {
+			delete(c.items, k)
+		}
+	}
+}
+
+// evictOldest removes the entry that is oldest based on expiry time.
+// If some entries have no expiry (zero time), the first such entry encountered
+// is considered the oldest. The function expects the write lock to be held.
+func (c *Cache) evictOldest() {
+	c.mu.Lock()
+	defer c.mu.Unlock()
+	c.evictOldestLocked()
+}
+
+func (c *Cache) evictOldestLocked() {
+	var oldestKey string
+	var oldestTime time.Time
+	for k, it := range c.items {
+		// Zero expiry means it never expires; treat it as the oldest possible.
+		if it.expiry.IsZero() {
+			oldestKey = k
+			break
+		}
+		if oldestKey == \"\" || it.expiry.Before(oldestTime) {
+			oldestKey = k
+			oldestTime = it.expiry
+		}
+	}
+	if oldestKey != \"\" {
+		delete(c.items, oldestKey)
+	}
+}
+
